# sam

> Persistent memory for coding agents. Watches your coding sessions, builds structured
> searchable memory, serves it via MCP. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code,
> and any MCP-compatible agent. Cross-machine sync included.

## Install

```bash
uv tool install synix-agent-mesh
sam setup    # detects agents, configures MCP + behavioral rules
sam serve    # starts memory server (mesh :7433, MCP :8200)
sam doctor   # verify everything works
```

`sam setup` is safe to run multiple times â€” never overwrites, only appends. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

## Key Concepts

- **Sessions**: Raw coding conversations (JSONL files from Claude Code, etc.)
- **Episodes**: LLM-summarized individual conversations
- **Rollups**: Weekly and monthly summaries synthesized from episodes
- **Synthesis**: Distilled context document + work status report
- **Memory**: The searchable index of all processed content
- **MCP**: Protocol for exposing memory tools to coding agents
- **Mesh**: Cross-device session sync and artifact distribution

## CLI

| Command | Effect | Safe |
|---------|--------|------|
| `sam setup` | Detect agents, configure MCP + behavioral rules | Yes (idempotent) |
| `sam setup --dry-run` | Preview setup without writing | Yes (read-only) |
| `sam doctor` | Health check with fix suggestions | Yes (read-only) |
| `sam serve` | Start memory server | Yes (Ctrl+C to stop) |
| `sam build --local` | Process sessions into memory | Yes (incremental) |
| `sam search "query"` | Search from command line | Yes (read-only) |
| `sam join --server HOST:PORT` | Join mesh as client | Confirm first |
| `sam sources add NAME DIR` | Add a data source | Yes |
| `sam sources list` | List configured sources | Yes (read-only) |

## MCP Tools (20 total)

### Read (safe, autonomous)
`search`, `get_artifact`, `get_flat_file`, `list_artifacts`, `list_layers`,
`list_releases`, `show_release`, `lineage`, `list_refs`, `open_project`, `source_list`

### Write (confirm first use)
`init_project`, `load_pipeline`, `build`, `release`,
`source_add_text`, `source_add_file`, `source_remove`

### Destructive (always confirm)
`clean`, `source_clear`

## Agent Behavioral Integration

`sam setup` injects instructions into each agent's behavioral config:

| Agent | File | Mode |
|-------|------|------|
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Append |
| Cursor | `~/.cursor/rules/sam-memory.mdc` | Create |
| Windsurf | `~/.codeium/windsurf/.windsurfrules` | Append |
| Roo Code | `~/.roo/rules.md` | Append |

These instructions tell the agent to:
1. Call `memory_search` with the project name at session start
2. Call `memory_get_flat_file("context-doc")` for synthesized project context
3. Call `memory_get_flat_file("work-status-doc")` for active work status
4. Search memory when encountering unfamiliar code or past decisions
5. Never call `memory_clean` or `memory_source_clear` without user confirmation
